Package org.eventb.core

Interface IPOSelectionHint

All Superinterfaces:
org.eclipse.core.runtime.IAdaptable, IElementManipulation, IInternalElement, IParent, IPOHint, IRodinElement, ISnapshotable
All Known Implementing Classes:
POSelectionHint
public interface IPOSelectionHint extends IPOHint
A selection hint points a prover at hypotheses that are most likely useful in proving a particular proof obligation. A selection hint can describe either a single predicate or an interval of predicates (determined by segment path of the tree of predicate sets).

If getEnd() evaluates to null, then a selection hint is a predicate selection hint, and getPredicate() yields the predicate.

If getEnd() does not evaluate to null, then a selection is an interval selection hint, where getEnd() denotes the last predicate set contained in the interval and getStart() denotes the predecessor of the first predicate set contained in the interval.

The following Java template shows how a selection can be read: 
        IPOPredicateSet endP = selectionHint.getEnd();
        if (endP == null) {
                IPOPredicate pred = selectionHint.getPredicate();
                ... // do something with pred
        } else {
                IPOPredicateSet startP = selectionHint.getStart();
                while (!endP.equals(startP)) {
                        IPOPredicate[] preds = endP.getPredicates();
                        for (IPOPredicate pred : preds) {
                                ... // do something with pred
                        }
                        endP = endP.getParentPredicateSet();
                }
        }

References to predicates and predicate sets are stored relative to plain PO files and translated back and forth, see IPORoot.

Since:
1.0

    IPOPredicateSet

    Returns the last predicate set contained in the interval or null.

    Returns the predicate handle in case the hint is a predicate selection hint.

    Returns the predecessor of the first predicate set contained in the interval.

  • Field Details

    • ELEMENT_TYPE

      static final IInternalElementType<IPOSelectionHint> ELEMENT_TYPE

  • Method Details

    • getPredicate

      IPOPredicate getPredicate() throws org.eclipse.core.runtime.CoreException
      Returns the predicate handle in case the hint is a predicate selection hint.
      Returns:
      the predicate handle
      Throws:
      org.eclipse.core.runtime.CoreException - if there was a problem accessing the database, or this is not a predicate selection hint

    • setPredicate

      void setPredicate(IPOPredicate predicate, org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor) throws org.eclipse.core.runtime.CoreException
      Makes this a predicate selection hint, and sets the predicate.
      Parameters:
      predicate - the predicate of the hint
      monitor - a progress monitor, or null if progress reporting is not desired
      Throws:
      org.eclipse.core.runtime.CoreException - if there was a problem accessing the database, or if the predicate was invalid

    • getStart

      IPOPredicateSet getStart() throws org.eclipse.core.runtime.CoreException
      Returns the predecessor of the first predicate set contained in the interval.
      Returns:
      the predecessor of the first predicate set contained in the interval
      Throws:
      org.eclipse.core.runtime.CoreException - if there was a problem accessing the database, or this is not an interval selection hint

    • getEnd

      IPOPredicateSet getEnd() throws org.eclipse.core.runtime.CoreException
      Returns the last predicate set contained in the interval or null. If the returned value is null, then this is a predicate selection hint.

      Use this method to determine whether this is a predicate or an interval selection hint.

      Returns:
      the last predicate set contained in the interval
      Throws:
      org.eclipse.core.runtime.CoreException - if there was a problem accessing the database, or this is not an interval selection hint

    • setInterval

      void setInterval(IPOPredicateSet start, IPOPredicateSet end, org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor) throws org.eclipse.core.runtime.CoreException
      Makes this an interval selection hint, and sets the interval.
      Parameters:
      start - the predecessor of the first predicate set contained in the interval
      end - the last predicate set contained in the interval
      monitor - a progress monitor, or null if progress reporting is not desired
      Throws:
      org.eclipse.core.runtime.CoreException - if there was a problem accessing the database, or this is not an interval selection hint
